texto: You grieve at first. And then slowly, with the yawning of the years, the disappeared gets scraped from your memory, the way your flesh can be peeled from your limbs. It's very harsh and extremely painful. But it gets done, square inch-by-square inch. Until, the skin that is your memory gets completely scarred and numbed. You live. The disappeared is detached from the dermis of remembering. And that is what is known as moving on.

texto: Not enough youths fighting windmills. And the old are fearful, jaded or dead. Do not ask me what to do. I am just as cowardly as you. And do not tell me it is enough to speak the truth; that it is bravery enough. Every mountain leveled to the ground, every forest burned, every man, woman, and child who lost their shanties to arsonist fires were defended to the heavens—with words.
explicacao: Citação de Psyche Roxas-Mendoza sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “Not enough youths fighting windmills. And the old are fearful, jaded or dead. Do not ask me what …” articula coragem, arsonist, bravery em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como termino e bravery.

texto: Guess life is really a road marked by crossroads. It's not the long and winding stretches that will hurt you the most. It's the crossroads. You make a bad choice and it's a tumble and a rumble before you get back on your feet again. But you'll get back on your feet. You'll find yourself. And then you'll be off to your next adventure (crossroads, I mean). In my case, I always had a safety net—my faith in God. But then, I'm more like the millions of simple folk who travel this world.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Psyche Roxas-Mendoza: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “Guess life is really a road marked by crossroads. It's not the long and winding stretches that wi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que fe, back, bad pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e bad.

texto: He was a son of the revolutionary movement when he and the revolutionary movement were still pristine. It was a special time for Filipino activists—a time when a hundred flowers bloomed and a thousand thoughts contended in a movement that did not know yet the price of betrayal from within. But flowers wilt and thoughts give way to rancor with the passing of years. And so some may grieve not his passing, while others fall to the ground in tears./FOR HORACIO BOY MORALES, JR. (September 11, 1943 – February 29, 2012)
explicacao: Citação de Psyche Roxas-Mendoza sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “He was a son of the revolutionary movement when he and the revolutionary movement were still pris…” articula tempo, activists, grieving em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como termino e grieving.

texto: The world is old and full of lessons. We will not lack for examples. We only have to look around carefully, earnestly, so we can learn and realize that success, to be genuine, must not be propelled by greed.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Psyche Roxas-Mendoza sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “The world is old and full of lessons. We will not lack for examples. We only have to look around …” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, greed, lessons, útil em momentos de inspiracao e estudos.

texto: Writers, when they’re good, open windows to worlds held precious and priceless by the soul. It is a sad day when they leave the earth, like having the windows shut for good. Where will the world be without good writers?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Psyche Roxas-Mendoza: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. texto: Beware! Balance rules the cosmos. It is not concerned with good or bad. You can be struck by misfortune and be buried in grief if that is what it takes to restore the imbalance you have wrought unto the world.
